2603.17673.title: Post-Training Local LLM Agents for Linux Privilege Escalation with Verifiable Rewards
2603.17673.paper_summary: LLM agents are increasingly relevant to research domains such as vulnerability discovery. Yet, the strongest systems remain closed and cloud-only, making them resource-intensive, difficult to reproduce, and unsuitable for work involving proprietary code or sensitive data. Consequently, there is an urgent need for small, local models that can perform security tasks under strict resource budgets, but methods for developing them remain underexplored. In this paper, we address this gap by proposing a two-stage post-training pipeline. We focus on the problem of Linux privilege escalation, where success is automatically verifiable and the task requires multi-step interactive reasoning. Using an experimental setup that prevents data leakage, we post-train a 4B model in two stages: supervised fine-tuning on traces from procedurally generated privilege-escalation environments, followed by reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards. On a held-out benchmark of 12 Linux privilege-escalation scenarios, supervised fine-tuning alone more than doubles the baseline success rate at 20 rounds, and reinforcement learning further lifts our resulting model, PrivEsc-LLM, to 95.8%, nearly matching Claude Opus 4.6 at 97.5%. At the same time, the expected inference cost per successful escalation is reduced by over 100x.
2603.17673.paper_summary_zh: LLM 代理在漏洞發現等研究領域變得越來越重要。<lb/>然而，最強大的系統仍然是封閉且僅限於雲端的，這使得它們資源密集、難以重現，且不適合涉及專有代碼或敏感數據的工作。<lb/>因此，迫切需要小型本地模型，能在嚴格的資源預算下執行安全任務，但開發這些模型的方法仍然未被充分探索。<lb/>在本文中，我們通過提出一個兩階段的後訓練流程來填補這一空白。<lb/>我們專注於 Linux 權限提升的問題，該問題的成功可以自動驗證，且任務需要多步互動推理。<lb/>使用一個防止數據洩漏的實驗設置，我們在兩個階段對一個 4B 模型進行後訓練：首先是在程序生成的權限提升環境的痕跡上進行監督微調，然後是使用可驗證獎勵的強化學習。<lb/>在一個保留的 12 個 Linux 權限提升場景的基準測試中，僅監督微調就使基準成功率在 20 輪中增長超過兩倍，而強化學習進一步將我們的模型 PrivEsc-LLM 提升至 95.8%，幾乎與 Claude Opus 4.6 的 97.5% 相匹配。<lb/>同時，每次成功的權限提升所需的預期推理成本降低了超過 100 倍。
2603.17673.author: Philipp Normann et.al.
2603.17673.authors: Philipp Normann, Andreas Happe, Jürgen Cito, Daniel Arp
